title: scutellaria orientalis subsp. macrostegia
intro: Scutellaria orientalis subsp. macrostegia, also known as Baikal skullcap, is a subspecies of the Scutellaria orientalis plant, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the mountainous regions of Siberia and the Russian Far East. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to have a positive impact on various health conditions. The health benefits of Scutellaria orientalis subsp. macrostegia include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and even exhibit neuroprotective effects. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or dried extract, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine and modern supplements.
